chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No. 629 of 2024 ( Mateen Ahmed Khan versus The State) Date Order with signature of Judges For hearing of bail application 29.04.2024 Mr. Shaneel Memon, advocate for the applicant Mr. Abrar Ali Khichi, Additional Prosecutor General for the State Complainant, Sohail Ibrahim, in person ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant and others issued two cheques worth rupees twenty-two lacs in favour of complainant Sohail Ibrahim, which were bounced by the concerned bank when were presented there for encashment, for which the present case was registered. The applicant, on refusal of pre-arrest bail by learned XIII- Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi East, has sought the same from this Court by making the instant bail application u/s 498 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The FIR of the incident has been lodged with a delay of more than one month; such delay could not be overlooked. The parties are disputed over the sale and purchase of the property. The subject cheques have not been issued by the applicant. The offence alleged against the applicant does not fall within the prohibitory clause. The punishment which the alleged offence entails is imprisonment for three years or a fine; if the applicant after the due trial is awarded the [ 2 ] punishment of fine only then the imprisonment which he is likely to undergo on account of the refusal of pre-arrest bail to him would be somewhat extra. The case has finally been challaned. The applicant has joined the trial and there is no allegation of misusing the concession of interim pre-arrest bail on his part.

The case has finally been challaned. The applicant has joined the trial and there is no allegation of misusing the concession of interim pre-arrest bail on his part. In these circumstances, a case for grant of pre-arrest bail to the applicant on the point of further inquiry and malafide is made out. In view of the above, while relying upon the case of Noman Khaliq v. the State (2023 SCMR 2122), the interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the applicant is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E
